steps.title: Identify the grammatical elements
steps.content: The blank falls between “appear” and “US currency.” The word “on” is a preposition, and “US currency” is the noun phrase that serves as its object.

steps.title: Apply the rule for prepositions and their objects
steps.content:
A preposition must connect directly to its object without intervening punctuation.

Choice D keeps “on” immediately before “US currency,” following this rule perfectly.

Choices A, B, and C each introduce punctuation (a semicolon, comma, or colon) that separates the preposition from its object, violating the requirement.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
